cards.title: Shipped native LLM & embedding plumbing
cards.description: Designed [Intuit](#project-intuit), a D library that wraps OpenAI-compatible and Claude endpoints behind persistent `Model` objects — streaming chat, single and batch embeddings, and per-model config. Ships AVX-optimized `cosine`, `dot`, and `L2` primitives in `intuit.space` for embedding-heavy retrieval.

cards.title: Unified scientific retrieval under one type
cards.description: Built [Akashi](#project-akashi) to pull PubChem, Wikipedia, PsychonautWiki, PubMed, and PMC behind a shared `Page` type, with a wikitext/XML parser that emits a compact AST and cross-source resolution (PubChem → wiki, similarity by CID). Written up in [Building Akashi](/articles/chemica-api).

cards.title: Cut manual annotation effort by ~95%
cards.description: Scripted Handshake AI's annotation pipeline with [AutoHandshake](#project-autohandshake) — Selenium driven like a dispatch runtime, resilient selectors, session recovery, and audit trails. Backed by Handshake's [Model Validation 2 — Expert](#certs-section) credential.

cards.title: Reverse-engineered Webull into a typed SDK
cards.description: Turned the Webull OpenAPI into [Webull-SDK](#project-webull-sdk): first-class `Security` objects with auto-updating market data, HMAC-SHA1 request signing, MFA-polling auth, and the full read-side surface — OHLCV bars, snapshots, L2 depth, ticks, and delta-volume footprints.

cards.title: Reached into the CLR from managed code
cards.description: Modeled CoreCLR internals — `MethodTable`, `EEClass`, `MethodDesc`, `FieldDesc`, VTable layout — as first-class C# types in [Cassowary](#project-cassowary), enabling allocation, casting, and boxing without dropping to native code. Ported to D as [Godwit](#project-godwit); dynamic syscall stubs explored in [Hacking C#](/articles/syscall-hooks).

cards.title: Credentialed across AI, security, and data
cards.description: Stacked Microsoft Career Essentials in [Generative AI, Software Development, Azure AI, and Security](#certs-section), plus NASBA SQL Programming, KNIME Data Science, PMI GitHub Career Essentials, and CompTIA Full-Stack Web Dev — grounding the [skills](#skills) I ship with.
